title: CFEC Releases Results of October 2025 Foreign Exchange Volume Survey
summary: The Canadian Foreign Exchange Committee released results of its October 2025 semi-annual FX volume survey covering eight major banks in Canada. Average daily turnover of traditional FX products (spot, outright forwards, FX swaps) increased 15.2% to US$231.7 billion from April 2025, while foreign exchange derivatives turnover decreased 31.6% to US$18.9 billion per day over the same period. Year-over-year, traditional FX products rose 13.2% while derivatives fell 2.6%.
key_points: Monthly turnover of traditional FX products totaled US$5.1 trillion in October 2025, with average daily turnover of US$231.7 billion (up 15.2% from April 2025)
Spot transactions decreased 16.0% to US$27.0 billion avg daily; outright forwards increased 5.0% to US$25.4 billion; FX swaps increased 23.9% to US$179.3 billion
FX derivatives monthly turnover totaled US$415.6 billion; average daily turnover US$18.9 billion, down 31.6% from April 2025
Currency swaps averaged US$13.9 billion daily (down 34.8%) and currency options averaged US$5.0 billion daily (down 20.6%)
Year-over-year comparison: traditional FX products up 13.2%, FX derivatives down 2.6%
Survey covers eight major banks: BofA Canada, Scotiabank, BMO, CIBC, National Bank, RBC, State Street, TD Securities
why_it_matters: This semi-annual survey provides benchmark data on the size and structure of Canada's FX market, tracked by the Bank of Canada as CFEC chair. The shift from derivatives to swaps in traditional products and the significant growth in FX swaps (up 23.9%) suggests dealers are favoring simpler hedging instruments. The 31.6% drop in derivatives turnover could reflect reduced volatility expectations or shift to OTC bilateral trading.
market_structure_context: Survey provides transparency on FX market structure by product type (spot, outright forwards, FX swaps, currency swaps, options), currency pairs, counterparty categories, maturity buckets, and execution methods. FX swaps dominate traditional FX volume at US$179.3B/day (77.4% of traditional FX turnover), reflecting their role in short-term funding and hedging.
important_numbers: US$231.7 billion/day average FX turnover (traditional products)
15.2% increase in traditional FX turnover (vs April 2025)
